popularStory.headline: Edward Fiennes de Clinton
popularStory.description: Edward Fiennes de Clinton, 1st Earl of Lincoln, KG (1512 – 16 January 1584/85) was an English nobleman and Lord High Admiral.[1] was born at Scrivelsby in Lincolnshire, the son of Thomas Clinton, 8th …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Edward Clinton於1512年出生在英國林可爾史爾史可萊維利斯比 考特時，他的父親Thomas Clinton 8th Lord Clinton22歲，母親Joan Poynings20歲。他於1530年與Elizabeth Blount結婚。他們至少是2個女兒的父母。他於1550年在英國被列為he was appointed privy counsellor (p.c.)。他去世於1585年1月16日在英國倫敦享年73歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭波克夏溫莎女爵 卡素。

nameMeanings.name: Clinton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish (Louth, Westmeath) and Scottish: shortened form of   McClinton  .

English: habitational name, either from Glympton in Oxfordshire, named as ‘settlement (Old English tūn) on the Glym river’, a Celtic river name meaning ‘bright stream’, or from Glinton in Northamptonshire, early recorded as Clinton (named with an unrecorded Old English element akin to Middle Low German glinde ‘enclosure, fence’ + Old English tūn). The surname was taken to Ireland in or before the 16th century.

History: Charles Clinton (born 1690 in Longford, Ireland) organized a group of colonists and founded the settlement of Little Britain, Ulster County, NY, in 1731. His son George Clinton (1739–1812) was governor of NY (1777–95), and they had many prominent descendants. — An English aristocratic family called Clinton was founded by Geoffrey de Clinton (flourished 1130), who held lands at Glympton, Oxfordshire. He was chamberlain and treasurer to Henry I (1100–35).

variants.name: McClinton
meanings: Irish (northern) and Scottish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Gille Fhionntain (Scottish), Mac Giolla Fhionntáin (Irish) ‘son of the servant (i.e. devotee) of (Saint) Fionntan’, whose name has been explained as ‘the white ancient’.

nameMeanings.name: Edward
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Edward Clinton was born in 1512, in Scrivelsby Court, Lincolnshire, England, his father, Thomas Clinton 8th Lord Clinton, was 22 and his mother, Joan Poynings, was 20. He married Elizabeth Blount in 1530. They were the parents of at least 2 daughters. In 1550, at the age of 38, his occupation is listed as he was appointed privy counsellor (p.c.) in England. He died on 16 January 1585, in London, England, at the age of 73, and was buried in Windsor Castle, Berkshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Edward Clinton est né en 1512, à Scrivelsby Court, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, son père, Thomas Clinton 8th Lord Clinton, avait 22 ans et sa mère, Joan Poynings, avait 20 ans. Il a épousé Elizabeth Blount en 1530. Ils ont eu au moins 2 filles. En 1550, à l’âge de 38 ans, sa profession de son indiquée est he was appointed privy counsellor (p.c.) à Angleterre. Il est décédé le 16 janvier 1585, à Londres, Angleterre, à l’âge de 73 ans, et a été enterré à Windsor Castle, Berkshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

Lui sposato Elizabeth Blount nel 1530. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figlie. Nel 1550, all'età di 38 anni, la sua occupazione è indicata come he was appointed privy counsellor (p.c.) nel England. Morì il 16 gennaio 1585, nel London, England, all'età di 73 anni, e fu sepolto a Windsor Castle, Berkshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Edward Clintonが1512年にイギリスリンコルンシュアイアスクリベルスビー コウルトで生まれた時，父Thomas Clinton 8th Lord Clintonは22歳，母Joan Poyningsは20歳であった。1530年にElizabeth Blountと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の娘の親であった。1550年，38歳の時，イギリスでの職業はhe was appointed privy counsellor (p.c.)と記録されている。彼は1585年1月16日にイギリスロンドンで73歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスバークシュアイアウィンドソル カストルにて埋葬された。

ko.Tattershall Castle, Kirton in Lindsey, L: 영국 잉글랜드 링코실르 커턴 인 린지
ko.lifeSummary: Sir Edward Clinton(이)가 1512년 영국 링코실르 스크라이벨스비 쿠르트에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Thomas Clinton 8th Lord Clinton(은)는 22세였고, 그의 어머니 Joan Poynings(은)는 20세였다. 그는 1530년 Elizabeth Blount(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 1550년, 38세의 나이로 그의 직업은 영국에서 he was appointed privy counsellor (p.c.)이었다. 그는 1585년 1월 16일 영국 런던에서 73세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 버크실르 윈드소르 카스틀에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Edward Clinton nasceu em 1512, em Scrivelsby Court, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Thomas Clinton 8th Lord Clinton, tinha 22 anos e sua mãe, Joan Poynings, tinha 20 anos. Ele casou-se com Elizabeth Blount em 1530. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhas. Em 1550, com 38 anos, sua profissão aparece como he was appointed privy counsellor (p.c.) em Inglaterra. Ele faleceu em 16 de janeiro de 1585, em London, Inglaterra, com 73 anos, e foi sepultado em Windsor Castle, Berkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.Tattershall Castle, Kirton in Lindsey, L: киртон ин линдси, линколншир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Edward Clinton родился в 1512 г. в местности скривелсби коурт, линколншир, Англия, его отцу по имени Thomas Clinton 8th Lord Clinton было 22 года, а матери, которую звали Joan Poynings, 20. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth Blount в 1530 г. У них было по крайней мере 2 дочери. В 1550 году, в возрасте 38 лет,  он работал по профессии he was appointed privy counsellor (p.c.) в местности Англия. Он умер 16 января 1585 г. в местности Лондон, Англия в возрасте 73 лет и был похоронен в местности виндзор кастл, биркшир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Edward Clinton nació en 1512, en Scrivelsby Court, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Thomas Clinton 8th Lord Clinton, tenía 22 años y su mamá, Joan Poynings, tenía 20 años. Se casó con Elizabeth Blount en 1530. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijas. En 1550, a la edad de 38 años, su ocupación registrada era he was appointed privy counsellor (p.c.) en Inglaterra. Murió el 16 de enero de 1585, en London, Inglaterra, a la edad de 73 años, y fue sepultado en Windsor Castle, Berkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
